title: Context, Cancellation, Backpressure, Structured Concurrency, and Leaks · Runtime Recall
summary: A request launches several operations; one fails, one blocks, one outlives the caller, and overload grows without bound. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a cancelable bounded worker group after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pass context as the first operation parameter across call boundaries and avoid storing it as durable domain state.
Derive, propagate, call, and observe cancellation, deadlines, timeouts, and causes while releasing timer resources.
Use context values only for request-scoped cross-boundary metadata with collision-safe keys, not optional parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Goroutine Leak Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
review case — Request 3 launches validation, lookup, and audit work under a 60ms deadline. One stage blocks after cancellation and the input queue grows. Bound concurrency, propagate cause, choose overload behavior, and prove no goroutine remains.

Inspect the Goroutine Leak Diagnosis trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Goroutine Leak Diagnosis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for detect leaked goroutines with ownership traces, cancellation tests, profiles, and changed failure cases. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Goroutine Leak Diagnosis, keep Goroutine Leak Diagnosis connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: For Goroutine Leak Diagnosis, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

One stage blocks after cancellation and the input queue grows. Bound concurrency, propagate cause, choose overload behavior, and prove no goroutine remains.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Goroutine Leak Diagnosis, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A stable process response proves no goroutines are leaking.
